1: Read it when choosing what shapes your mind — meditate on Scripture, not scoffers
A picture of steady fruitfulness: stay planted by God's word, not blown like chaff
A sober warning: the ungodly's path ends in judgment, not just missed blessing
2: Pray it when Christ's authority is mocked or resisted — his kingship still stands
A call to submit to Christ now, in reverent joy, rather than face him as judge
Comfort when the world conspires against the church: God laughs, his king reigns
3: Pray it when trouble surrounds you and you can't sleep for fear
A model for turning fear into confident rest: salvation belongs to the LORD
Say it aloud when enemies or troubles multiply, to steady your heart
4: Pray it at night when anxious or slandered — settle your heart before sleep
Answer accusers by being still before God, not retaliating
Lie down in peace: the LORD alone keeps you safe, even amid conflict
5: Pray it each morning as your first request to God for direction
Ask to be led in a straight path past those who mean you harm
Take comfort: those who take refuge in the LORD end up shouting for joy
6: Pray it in seasons of grief or bodily weakness mixed with guilt over sin
Bring nightly tears honestly to God rather than hiding your distress
Take hope: even mid-lament, trust that the LORD has heard your weeping
7: Pray it when falsely accused — ask God, the righteous judge, to vindicate you
A warning: those who dig a pit for others often fall into it themselves
Bring specific charges against you to God rather than taking revenge
8: Pray it gazing at the night sky, marveling that God is mindful of you
A reminder that your dignity as a person is God-given, not earned
Worship prompt: creation's vastness should deepen awe, not despair
9: Pray it when longing for justice — thank God he judges the nations rightly
Comfort for the oppressed: the LORD has not forgotten the cry of the humble
A model for turning thanksgiving into confidence for hard days ahead
10: Pray it when the wicked seem to prosper unchecked and God seems distant
Cry out for the fatherless and poor — ask God to defend the defenseless
Remember: God sees, even when the arrogant insist he does not
11: Pray it when tempted to run from danger instead of trusting God
Remember the LORD reigns from his throne even when foundations shake
Rest in this: God tests all people but delights in the upright
12: Pray it in a culture of flattery and lies — cling to God's pure words instead
A warning against trusting boastful, deceptive speech
Comfort when the faithful seem scarce: God's promises are refined like silver
13: Pray it when God feels absent — voice your 'how long' honestly
A pattern: move from raw complaint to deliberate trust before relief comes
Resolve to sing to the LORD even while still waiting for the answer
14: Pray it when surrounded by practical atheism or corruption
A sober check: examine your own heart for functional denial of God
Close longing for salvation to come, not despair, at universal sinfulness
15: Use it as a checklist for integrity — truthful speech, kept promises, no bribery
Ask before worship: is my daily conduct fit to dwell with God?
A reminder that ordinary honesty, not religious performance, marks godliness
16: Pray it when tempted to seek security elsewhere — the LORD is your portion
Comfort in facing death: God will not abandon his own to the grave
Points to Christ's resurrection — read it to anchor hope beyond the grave
17: Pray it inviting God to test your heart for hidden sin
Ask to be kept 'as the apple of the eye' when you feel exposed to danger
Hope for the last day: waking satisfied to see God's likeness
18: Pray it after deliverance from real danger — give God, not yourself, the credit
A model of vivid, grateful praise after answered prayer
Trust God's mercy extends to you and your household, as it did David's
19: Pray it outdoors — let creation turn your heart to praise before Scripture
Read Scripture as sweeter than honey, not just duty — it converts the soul
Close by asking God to cleanse faults you don't even notice in yourself
20: Pray it for leaders and loved ones facing a hard trial or decision
Put trust in God's name rather than resources or strategy
Intercede specifically — name the person and the need before God
21: Give thanks for blessings already received before asking for more
Pray with confidence that God will finish what he has begun
A model for pairing patient prayer (Ps 20) with grateful praise
22: Pray it in the darkest suffering — even Christ prayed these words
Move from raw lament toward a vow to praise, even before rescue comes
Read it at the Lord's Table to see the cross foretold in detail
23: Pray it in fear or grief — the Shepherd is present even in the valley
A daily reminder that God provides rest and guidance, not just rescue
Comfort for the dying: goodness and mercy follow you all your days
24: Pray it before worship, examining your hands and heart before entering
Remember everything you own belongs first to the LORD
Welcome the King of glory into your life, not just your church building
25: Pray it when unsure which way to go — ask God to teach you his paths
Confess youthful sins specifically and trust God to forget them
Rest in God's covenant kindness rather than your own record
26: Invite God to examine your heart rather than assuming you're fine
Choose your company deliberately — keep distance from evildoers
Ground confidence in love for God's presence, not personal sinlessness
27: Pray it when afraid — the LORD is light and salvation against any threat
Bring raw longing to God even when family or friends fail you
Close by choosing to wait on the LORD rather than force an answer
28: Pray it when tempted to go along with wrongdoing around you
Ask specifically not to be swept away with those who do evil
Let answered prayer turn instantly into thanksgiving
29: Pray it during a storm, literal or figurative — God's voice is over it
Worship prompt: let displays of raw power turn you to reverence
Rest in this: the same mighty God gives his people strength and peace
30: Pray it after recovering from illness or a close call — give thanks aloud
A warning against letting prosperity breed false self-sufficiency
Hold onto this in hard nights: weeping lodges a night, joy comes morning
31: Pray 'into thine hand I commit my spirit' when facing danger or death
Bring failing strength and isolation honestly to God
Move from complaint to trust without pretending the pain is gone
32: Confess sin honestly and promptly — don't let guilt fester in your body
Pray it after confession to feel the relief of forgiveness
A warning: don't be stubborn like a horse or mule that must be forced right
33: Pray it when anxious about world events — the word that made the heavens rules nations
Trust God's plans over political or military strength
Wait on the LORD as shield and help rather than scheming your own way
34: Pray it when afraid — 'taste and see that the LORD is good'
Practice the fear of the LORD as the path to a genuinely good life
Comfort for the brokenhearted: God is near to the crushed in spirit
35: Pray it honestly when betrayed by a friend, rather than taking revenge
Ask God to fight for you like a warrior when you are outmatched
A model for bringing raw anger over injustice directly to God
36: Pray it when the wicked seem to plot even in their sleep — God's love is bigger
Take shelter under God's wings when overwhelmed by others' schemes
Let the scale of God's faithfulness dwarf your fear of wrongdoers
37: Read it when tempted to envy the prospering wicked — don't fret, trust
Practice quiet good-doing instead of aggressive self-assertion
Hold onto the promise: the meek, not the pushy, inherit the land
38: Pray it when suffering and sin feel tangled together — confess honestly
Don't wait for friends' support before turning to God
End in hope: confession leads to a plea God is willing to answer
39: Pray it to gain perspective on how few your days are
Practice holding your tongue before venting frustration
Ask for strength as 'a stranger and a sojourner' passing through
40: Give thanks for a specific time God lifted you out of a pit
Choose obedience over religious performance — 'I delight to do thy will'
Bring fresh troubles to God even while still thanking him for past rescue
41: Care practically for the poor and weak — it carries a promised blessing
Pray it when betrayed by someone close, naming the pain honestly
Close a season of hard prayer with deliberate praise, like Book One does
42: Pray it when you feel spiritually dry or cut off from worship
Preach to your own soul: 'why art thou cast down... hope thou in God'
Let remembered seasons of worship fuel hope in a hard season now
43: Pray it asking God to guide you back to worship when you feel distant
Ask for God's light and truth specifically as your guides
Repeat the refrain 'hope thou in God' until your soul answers
44: Pray it corporately when the church suffers despite faithfulness
Resist explaining unexplained suffering — just cry, 'Arise, help us'
Recall God's past faithfulness to fuel present corporate prayer
45: Read it to see Christ's eternal throne and beauty exalted
A call to leave old loyalties behind for wholehearted devotion to Christ
Use it at a wedding to point the couple toward Christ's greater marriage
46: Pray it when the world feels like it's falling apart — God is refuge
Practice actual stillness before God rather than anxious striving
Hold onto this in crisis: nations rage, but God stills them with a word
47: Pray it as praise for Christ's kingship over every nation, not just yours
Clap and shout — let this psalm shape exuberant, not somber, worship
Remember God's rule extends beyond your own people or church
48: Pray it giving thanks for the security God gives his church
Walk through and 'count the towers' — take stock of God's faithfulness
Pass on to the next generation the specific ways God has protected you
49: Read it when tempted to trust wealth for security — it can't buy back a life
Remember the rich and poor share the same grave — plan accordingly
Ground hope in God's power to redeem your soul, not your bank account
50: Examine whether your worship is genuine thanksgiving or empty ritual
A warning against reciting God's word while living in disobedience
Remember God doesn't need your service — he wants your heart
51: Pray it when confessing a specific, named sin, not vague guilt
Ask God to create a clean heart and renew a right spirit
Comfort: a broken and contrite heart he will never despise
52: Read when tempted to envy the ruthless and successful
Warning: boasting in evil and lies invites God's downfall
Anchor your security in God's house, not wealth or power
53: Read as a sober reminder that no one is righteous on their own
Prompts prayer for revival and salvation for God's people
Warning against practical atheism, living as if God isn't watching
54: Pray it when someone you trusted has betrayed you to an enemy
Model: name God as your helper before the rescue even comes
Respond to deliverance with a specific offering of thanks
55: Pray it when a close friend, not a stranger, has betrayed you
Instead of fleeing, commit to fixed times of daily prayer
Comfort: cast your burden on the LORD and he will sustain you
56: Pray this when fear is present but you still choose to trust
Comfort: God keeps a record of every tear you've cried
Trust doesn't require the absence of fear, just a choice
57: Pray it surrounded by real danger or hostile people
Practice deliberately turning lament into praise mid-prayer
Resolve to greet the morning with worship, not dread
58: Pray it when officials or judges abuse power for injustice
Entrust corrupt authorities to God's judgment, not your own
Comfort: God's justice will be made visible, not hidden forever
59: Pray it when you feel hunted or watched by real enemies
Ask for lessons to be learned, not just quick destruction
End the night in fear, wake singing of God's strength
60: Pray it as a community after a shared defeat or setback
Remember God owns the outcome even when help seems absent
Warning against trusting human strength over God's power
61: Pray it when you feel far from help and overwhelmed
Ask God to lead you to a security higher than yourself
Intercede for your leaders' long life and faithful reign
62: Read to recenter trust on God alone, not mixed loyalties
Warning: don't trust extortion, riches, or human status
Repeat 'God alone' when tempted to hedge your trust
63: Pray it in a spiritually dry season, longing for God's presence
Practice meditating on God during sleepless night hours
Seek satisfaction in God himself, not circumstances
64: Pray it when gossip or slander is aimed at you in secret
Trust God to expose and reverse hidden schemes against you
Comfort: the righteous will be vindicated, not the slanderer
65: Give thanks for forgiveness and for God's provision together
Pray it at harvest time or when thankful for daily provision
See God's rule over nature as evidence of his care for you
66: Recall a specific way God has tested and refined your faith
Pair public praise with a personal testimony of answered prayer
Fulfill vows or promises you made when God helped you
67: Pray for blessing as a means to make God known, not an end
Use it to pray for missions and the nations' knowledge of God
Repeat the refrain: let all the peoples praise God together
68: Pray it when you need a defender, God cares for the fatherless
Praise God as one who actively fights for the vulnerable
Read the ascension language as pointing forward to Christ
69: Pray it when suffering comes specifically for being faithful
Read it alongside Christ's passion, he bore this too
Hand imprecation over to God rather than acting on it yourself
70: Use it as a short, urgent prayer in a real crisis
Don't wait for eloquence, cry out 'make haste' plainly
A model for prayer when mockery adds to your distress
71: Pray it in old age, or for the elderly, against being forgotten
Resolve to keep testifying to the next generation, whatever your age
Comfort for fading strength: God has kept you since birth
72: Pray it for civil leaders, that they defend the poor and needy
See it as a picture of Christ's just and universal reign
Marks the close of Book II, a fitting benediction to pray
73: Pray it when envying the ease of the wealthy or ungodly
Regain perspective in corporate worship, not isolated brooding
Comfort: God himself, not circumstances, is your true portion
74: Pray it when a church or place of worship is attacked or defiled
Appeal to God's past faithfulness as grounds for present hope
Grieve corporately, this is a prayer for the whole community
75: Read it when tempted to grasp for promotion or status
Trust that God alone raises up and brings down
Comfort: injustice will not go unanswered forever
76: Praise God as sovereign over even hostile human wrath
Give thanks for specific, remembered deliverances or victories
Approach God with appropriate fear alongside gratitude
77: Pray it during insomnia or sleepless anxious nights
Deliberately recall specific past evidences of God's faithfulness
Let remembered history, not present feeling, settle your doubt
78: Use it to teach children God's faithfulness through real history
See your own forgetfulness reflected honestly in Israel's cycle
Trust God's patient faithfulness despite generations of failure
79: Pray it after national tragedy or the destruction of a holy place
Ask for mercy honestly, without excusing past sin
Vow to keep giving thanks even out of grief and loss
80: Pray the refrain when longing for revival and God's favor
Picture yourself as a vine needing God's continued tending
Look to Christ, the 'son of man,' as the source of restoration
81: Pray it at times of corporate worship and festival gladness
Let it prompt self-examination: are you actually listening to God?
Regret missed blessing that comes from simple obedience
82: Pray it when officials fail to protect the poor and vulnerable
Remember earthly authority is accountable and mortal
Ask God himself to be the final judge of injustice
83: Pray it when facing coordinated opposition to God's people
Ask that God's judgment lead enemies to know him, not just lose
Recall past deliverances as precedent for present prayer
84: Pray it when you miss corporate worship or feel far from church
Choose a lesser place in God's house over comfort in the world
Let longing for worship, not obligation, draw you there
85: Pray it when you long for spiritual revival, personal or corporate
Ask God to revive rather than assume his anger is permanent
Hope for the day mercy, truth, righteousness, and peace unite
86: Pray it honestly admitting you are poor and needy before God
Ask specifically for an undivided heart, not just relief
Appeal to God's mercy toward 'all who call on him'
87: Pray it with hope for the salvation of hostile or distant peoples
See the church as a city that welcomes former enemies as citizens
Take comfort: God's family is wider than your own background
88: Pray it when there is no resolution or silver lining in sight
Permission to bring raw despair to God without a tidy ending
Comfort: even unanswered lament is real prayer, not failure
89: Pray it when God's promises and present circumstances seem to clash
Hold onto covenant faithfulness even amid visible setback
It's honest to ask 'how long, LORD' without a resolved answer
90: Pray it at a funeral, birthday, or any reminder of mortality
Ask God to teach you to number your days toward wisdom
Let human brevity humble you rather than despair you
91: Pray it when facing danger, illness, or unseen threats
Dwell deliberately in God's presence, not just call on him in crisis
Comfort: God personally pledges to answer and deliver you
92: Pray it on the Lord's Day as a discipline of regular praise
Don't envy the wicked's quick rise, it doesn't last
Hope: righteous fruit-bearing continues into old age
93: Pray it when chaos or upheaval feels overwhelming
Rest in God's kingship being older and stronger than any crisis
Ground your stability in his throne, not circumstances
94: Pray it when injustice against the vulnerable seems unseen
Leave vengeance to God rather than taking it yourself
Comfort: the God who made your eyes and ears sees everything
95: Pray it to move from casual to kneeling, wholehearted worship
Warning: don't let today's worship become tomorrow's hard heart
Take the warning seriously, forfeited rest is a real danger
96: Pray it with a heart for missions and the nations' worship
Let creation's joy remind you praise is bigger than yourself
Anticipate God's righteous judgment as good news, not just dread
97: Pray it to reset your fear toward God's overwhelming majesty
Reject any idol or substitute you've let compete with him
Comfort: light and gladness are sown ahead for the upright
98: Sing it as thanks for a victory only God could have won
Let creation's joy, floods clapping, hills singing, shape your praise
Look forward to God's coming judgment as fair and right
99: Pray it holding together God's holiness and his mercy
Follow Moses, Aaron, Samuel's example: intercede boldly for others
Comfort: God answers intercessors even for a wayward people
100: Pray it as a simple call to gladness and thanksgiving daily
Remember: he made you, you belong to him as his sheep
Enter worship with thanksgiving, not just routine or duty
101: Pray it when tempted to act uprightly in public but loosely at home
Examine who you let close — refuse a seat at your table to deceit
A charge for leaders: private integrity must come before public rule
102: Pray it in sickness or loneliness when your strength is failing fast
Bring raw grief honestly to God rather than masking it
Rest in this: God's permanence outlasts your own frailty and years
103: Use it to deliberately recount God's specific mercies, not just feel grateful
Remember your own frailty ('we are but dust') without despair
A model for confession: he forgives all iniquity, not just some
104: Read it outdoors or in nature to turn creation's order into praise
Notice God's daily provision for animals as evidence of his care for you
Let it prompt gratitude for the ordinary rhythms of a sustained world
105: Read it to rehearse God's specific past faithfulness, not vague feelings
Retell this history to your children as it was meant to be retold
Let remembered promises steady you when present circumstances waver
106: Use it as a template for honest corporate confession, not excuse-making
Notice how patience from God met repeated failure — take courage
Pray it when your own community needs to own a pattern of sin
107: Pray it after any rescue — from danger, illness, or a hard season
Recognize God's hand in ordinary crises, not just dramatic ones
Practice the habit of thanking God specifically, not just generally
108: Pray it before a hard undertaking, pairing praise with a plea for help
Remember that no strategy or strength of yours secures the outcome
Start the day praising God before asking him for anything
109: Pray it when falsely accused — hand vengeance to God, not yourself
Resist retaliating against slander; bring it to God in prayer instead
A caution: don't take up the vengeance this psalm surrenders to God
110: Read it to see Christ's kingship and priesthood joined in one person
Worship Christ as the priest-king seated at God's right hand
Trace how the New Testament quotes this psalm about Jesus
111: Memorize it as a template: recount specific works of God, not generalities
Let 'the fear of the LORD is the beginning of wisdom' shape decisions
Praise God's faithfulness as something that endures, not fleeting
112: Ask whether your own life matches this portrait — generous, steady, unafraid
Let it steady you against bad news by trusting a fixed heart in God
Give generously and freely as evidence of fearing the LORD
113: Marvel that the exalted God stoops to notice the poor and needy
Pray it when you feel too small or low for God to bother with
Praise God's humility in bending down, not just his greatness
114: Recall the exodus to remember God's power is still active today
Let creation's trembling before God provoke your own reverent awe
Read it alongside Psalm 113 as one Passover-season pairing
115: Pray it when tempted to trust in things that can't see, hear, or save
Give God alone the glory rather than claiming credit yourself
Teach the next generation to trust and bless the LORD daily
116: Pray it after God answers a prayer — follow with a specific act of thanks
Keep vows made to God when you were desperate; don't forget them
Let 'I love the LORD' be grounded in a memory of being heard
117: Use its brevity as a quick call to worship any time, any place
Remember God's love reaches every nation, not just your own circle
Praise God for his enduring truth in a short, simple prayer
118: Pray it approaching worship, giving thanks that his mercy endures
See Christ as the rejected stone now the cornerstone — trust him
Repeat 'his mercy endureth for ever' as a steadying refrain
119: Read a stanza a day to build sustained delight in God's word
Bring every life situation, obedience, affliction, comfort, to Scripture
Let its length model patient, unhurried meditation on the Bible
120: Pray it when surrounded by lying speech or conflict-loving people
Long for peace even while living among those who love strife
Let it begin your own 'ascent' out of a hard place toward worship
121: Pray it when you feel unwatched — God neither slumbers nor sleeps
A travel/journey blessing: he keeps your going out and coming in
Look above the hills, not at them, for help
122: Pray it before corporate worship, glad for the chance to go up
Pray specifically for the peace of your church and city
Let pilgrimage joy remind you gathering with God's people is a gift
123: Pray it under mockery or contempt — wait rather than retaliate
Fix your eyes on God's hand the way a servant watches a master's
Practice patient dependence when scorn from others wears you down
124: Pray it after a narrow escape: credit God, not luck or your own skill
Say aloud 'if it had not been the LORD' before recounting deliverance
Remember you are not self-sufficient — help came from outside you
125: Pray it when circumstances feel shaky — trust God's own stability
Rest like Mount Zion: trust makes you immovable, not your effort
Ask God to keep wickedness from ruling over the righteous
126: Pray it in hardship, holding present tears alongside past deliverance
Sow in tears now, trusting a harvest of joy will follow
Remember a specific past rescue to fuel hope in a present one
127: Pray it before a big project — labor without God's blessing is wasted
Rest instead of anxious overwork; let God build what you're building
Receive children as a gift and reward, not merely a burden
128: Pray it for your household — ordinary family life flows from fearing God
Let reverence for God, not ambition, be what you build a home on
Give thanks for long life spent among family and community
129: Pray it in long affliction — endurance itself is a testimony
Take heart: repeated hardship has not overcome you, and won't
Trust that opposition to God's people withers like rootless grass
130: Pray it 'out of the depths' in guilt or despair — cry out, don't hide
Wait for the LORD's grace the way a watchman waits for dawn
Remember forgiveness, not merit, is why you can stand before God
131: Pray it to quiet restless ambition or anxious striving
Practice contentment: let go of matters too great for you
Rest in God like a weaned child rests quietly with its mother
132: Pray it recalling God's unbreakable promises, not your own effort
Let costly devotion to God's house shape your own priorities
Trust God's commitments outlast even a king's best intentions
133: Pray it for your church or family when unity feels fragile
Pursue peace with fellow believers; treat unity as a blessing to protect
Picture unity as oil and dew — flowing outward, not staying put
134: Pray it late at night, blessing God even in unseen, tired hours
Lift your hands in worship even when no one else is watching
Receive God's blessing back after offering him yours
135: Praise God's total freedom to act, not chained by circumstance
Refuse to trust dead idols — money, status — that cannot help you
Remember being chosen by God is his free gift, not your merit
136: Pray it responsively, answering each of God's works with thanks
Repeat 'his mercy endureth for ever' over both good days and hard ones
Recount God's specific deeds rather than praising in vague terms
137: Pray it when grief over loss feels too raw for polite words
Don't forget what matters most, even far from home or church
Bring even bitter anger to God rather than acting on it yourself
138: Give thanks publicly, not just privately, when God answers prayer
Take comfort that the exalted God still regards the lowly
Trust God to finish what concerns you, even work left undone
139: Pray it when you feel hidden or unknown — God already knows you fully
Invite God to search your heart and expose what you can't see
Take comfort that his presence reaches every place, even darkness
140: Pray it against slander or harassment — turn to God, not revenge
Trust God to unravel schemes set against you like hidden snares
Ask for guarded speech and protection in the day of conflict
141: Pray it in the evening, offering the day's prayer like incense
Ask God to guard your mouth before speaking in anger or haste
Welcome correction from a godly friend instead of resenting it
142: Pray it when isolated and no one seems to notice your struggle
Pour out your complaint honestly to God rather than bottling it up
Remember God himself is your refuge when human help runs out
143: Pray it in despair, moving from an overwhelmed spirit toward hope
Ask to be taught God's will when you don't know which way to turn
Remember no one is justified by merit — lean on grace, not record
144: Pray it before a hard task, asking God to train and strengthen you
Marvel that God notices you at all despite how brief your days are
Pray for your household's everyday needs, not just spiritual ones
145: Praise God's specific attributes daily: gracious, compassionate, near
Pass praise of God on to the next generation deliberately
Call on God in truth, trusting he is near to those who do
146: Pray it when tempted to put ultimate trust in a leader or institution
Care for the hungry, fatherless, and widow as God himself does
Locate your happiness in God, not in powerful people or plans
147: Praise God for both cosmic power and personal, tender care together
Bring a broken heart to God, who binds up wounds by name
Trust the same God who numbers stars also gathers the outcast
148: Join creation's total, universal call to praise God without exception
Let nothing, weather, animals, rulers, be exempt from your praise
Praise God simply because his name alone is exalted
149: Worship God with joy, remembering he delights in his people
Sing a new song rather than relying only on old routines
Trust that God's justice for the nations will ultimately be executed
150: End your own prayer time echoing 'let everything that hath breath praise'
Use varied ways to praise God — voice, instruments, whatever you have
Let this be the last word: praise is the fitting close to everything
